raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Kern County Board of Supervisors approved $2 million in state funding for Good Samaritan Hospital to expand addiction recovery services.",
    "Good Samaritan Hospital will provide in-patient detox facilities for Medi-Cal patients using funding from the Fentanyl Task Force.",
    "This program aims to offer specialized, intensive care to help patients stabilize and begin their recovery journey.",
    "The pilot program will provide timely inpatient medical detoxification treatment and tailored aftercare services for individuals struggling with substance use disorders.",
    "Five hospital beds will be reserved exclusively for Medi-Cal beneficiaries requiring 24-hour medical oversight.",
    "Patients will receive around-the-clock nursing care, regular check-ins with medical doctors and psychiatrists, and a combination of counseling, therapy, and educational sessions.",
    "No other programs in Kern County currently provide the level of care offered by this initiative.",
    "The opioid crisis has amplified the need for comprehensive detox services in the community.",
    "Expanding access to life-saving detox services is expected to reduce the strain on emergency services, hospitals, and law enforcement.",
    "AB 2376, a law authored by Dr. Bains in 2024, streamlines regulatory requirements for healthcare facilities to accept more patients for addiction treatments.",
    "The 2022 needs assessment from the California Department of Healthcare Services found over 10,000 overdose deaths annually, with more than 7,000 involving opioids and 83% linked to fentanyl."
  ]
}
```
